dns.description: Domain Name System - resolves hostnames to IPs
dns.notes: Zone transfer (AXFR) is high value. Check for open recursion on external DNS.

ftp.notes: Always test anonymous login first. Check for writable directories.

ssh.notes: Check for weak algorithms, deprecated ciphers, and key-based auth. Avoid aggressive brute force.

telnet.notes: Cleartext protocol — capture credentials if MitM is in scope.

commands: telnet TARGET 25 (then: EHLO test; VRFY root)
smtp.notes: Test for open relay, user enumeration via VRFY/EXPN/RCPT, and NTLM info disclosure.

pop3.description: Post Office Protocol v3 - email retrieval
commands: telnet TARGET 110 (then: USER admin; PASS admin)
pop3.notes: Port 995 is POP3S (TLS). Check for plaintext credentials on port 110.

imap.notes: Port 993 is IMAPS. Check for plaintext auth on port 143.

http.notes: Phase 1: fingerprint (curl/whatweb/nmap). Phase 2: known CVEs and misconfigs (nuclei, all tags in one pass). Phase 3: content discovery (ffuf). Follow data flow — every parameter is a finding candidate.

https.notes: Phase 1: TLS (sslscan/testssl.sh/sslyze/nmap-ssl). Phase 2: fingerprint app layer (curl/whatweb/nmap-http). Phase 3: known CVEs and misconfigs (nuclei, all tags in one pass). Phase 4: content discovery (ffuf).

netbios_ns.notes: Maps NetBIOS names to IPs. Useful for internal Windows host discovery.

netbios_ssn.notes: Legacy SMB over NetBIOS. Usually paired with port 445.

smb.description: Server Message Block - Windows file/printer sharing
smb.notes: Check for null sessions, guest access, EternalBlue (MS17-010), and SMB signing disabled.

msrpc.notes: Null session on RPC can expose domain users, groups, and policy. Map RPC services with rpcdump.

ldap.notes: Try anonymous/unauthenticated bind first. Dump AD objects with ldapdomaindump.

ldaps.notes: Port 3269 is Global Catalog over SSL. Check certificate validity.

commands: impacket-ticketer -nthash HASH -domain-sid S-1-5-21-... -domain DOMAIN -spn cifs/TARGET Administrator
kerberos.notes: AS-REP Roasting (no preauth required users), Kerberoasting (SPNs), and user enumeration via Kerberos.

rdp.notes: Check for BlueKeep (CVE-2019-0708), NLA disabled, and weak credentials.

winrm.notes: Port 5986 is HTTPS (WinRM over TLS). Evil-WinRM supports pass-the-hash.

wmi.notes: WMI uses dynamic high ports after initial connection to 135. Useful for lateral movement.

snmp.notes: Community strings 'public' and 'private' are common defaults. SNMPv3 has auth. Check for system info, routing tables, and user lists.

ntp.notes: NTP monlist can amplify DDoS and leak peer IPs. Check for misconfigured NTP servers.

tftp.notes: No authentication. Used by network devices for config/firmware. Try to fetch common config files.

finger.notes: Legacy protocol that can disclose logged-in users and account information.

mssql.notes: Check for sa blank password, xp_cmdshell enabled, and linked servers. UDP 1434 = SQL Browser service.

mysql.notes: Check for root with blank password, anonymous access, and privilege escalation via FILE privilege.

postgres.notes: Default user is 'postgres'. Check for COPY TO/FROM PROGRAM for code execution.

oracle.notes: Enumerate SIDs first (oracle-sid-brute). Default SIDs: XE, ORCL, ORACLE, PROD.

mongodb.notes: Older MongoDB versions have no auth by default. List all databases and collections.

redis.notes: Unauthenticated Redis can allow RCE via CONFIG SET (writing cron, SSH keys, or webshells).

elasticsearch.notes: Unauthenticated clusters can expose all data. Port 9300 is transport layer.

cassandra.notes: Port 9160 is Thrift API (deprecated). Check for no-auth and accessible keyspaces.

couchdb.notes: Admin Party mode gives unauthenticated admin access. CVE-2017-12635 for privilege escalation.

commands: nc TARGET 11211 (then: stats; stats items; stats cachedump SLABID LIMIT)
memcached.notes: No authentication. Can expose session data, credentials, or application secrets.

commands: cypher-shell -a bolt://TARGET:7687 -u neo4j -p neo4j 'MATCH (n) RETURN n LIMIT 10'
neo4j.notes: Default creds: neo4j/neo4j. HTTP API on 7474, Bolt protocol on 7687.

nfs.notes: Check for no_root_squash on exports. Can escalate via UID=0 file creation.

rsync.notes: Anonymous rsync can expose files or allow upload. Can overwrite SSH authorized_keys.

vnc.notes: Check for authentication bypass (CVE-2006-2369 realvnc), null auth, and weak passwords.

x11.notes: Unauthenticated X11 allows screen capture and keystroke logging.

cups.notes: CUPS 2.x CVE-2024-47176 (unauthenticated RCE). Enumerate printers and admin access.

commands: ipmitool -I lanplus -H TARGET -U admin -P admin chassis status
ipmitool -I lanplus -H TARGET -U '' -P '' cipher 0 chassis status
ipmi.notes: Cipher 0 auth bypass (CVE-2013-4786) leaks password hashes. IPMI 2.0 RAKP hash disclosure is critical.

sip.notes: Enumerate extensions with svwar. Check for unauthenticated calls and INVITE flooding.

rtsp.notes: IP cameras often use RTSP. Try default credentials (admin/admin, admin/blank).

commands: docker -H tcp://TARGET:2375 run -v /:/mnt --rm -it alpine chroot /mnt sh
docker.notes: Unauthenticated Docker API = full host compromise via privileged container mount. Port 2376 is TLS.

kubernetes.notes: Kubelet read-only port 10255 and anonymous API access are critical. Check for RBAC misconfigs.

etcd.notes: Unauthenticated etcd can expose all Kubernetes secrets including service account tokens.

grafana.notes: Default creds: admin/admin. CVE-2021-43798 path traversal for older versions.

prometheus.notes: Unauthenticated Prometheus exposes scrape targets, internal IPs, and metrics data.

alertmanager.notes: Can expose webhook URLs and notification configs (Slack tokens, PagerDuty keys).

consul.description: HashiCorp Consul service mesh and discovery
consul.notes: KV store may contain secrets, service configs, and tokens. Port 8600 is DNS interface.

vault.notes: Check seal status, enumerate secret paths with discovered tokens. Look for root token exposure.

jenkins.notes: Port 50000 is JNLP agent port. Check for unauthenticated script console (Groovy RCE).

gitlab.notes: Check for public repos, user enumeration, and version-specific CVEs. Default admin password prompt on first login.

sonarqube.notes: Default creds: admin/admin. Exposes source code, security findings, and secrets in scan results.

nexus.notes: Default creds: admin/admin123. Can host malicious packages for supply chain attacks.

rabbitmq.notes: Default creds: guest/guest (localhost only by default). Port 15672 = management UI.

kafka.notes: Port 2181 is ZooKeeper. Unauthenticated Kafka can expose sensitive message payloads.

zookeeper.notes: Can contain Kafka configs, HBase data, and application configs.

git_daemon.notes: Unauthenticated read access to git repos. May expose source code and credentials in history.

svn.notes: Anonymous SVN can expose source code. Check for .svn directories in web roots.

bgp.notes: Misconfigured BGP can allow route hijacking. Map AS numbers and BGP peers.

ldap_gc.notes: Global Catalog contains a partial replica of all objects in the forest. Port 3269 is GC over SSL.

dhcp.notes: Rogue DHCP can redirect traffic. DHCP starvation is a DoS vector.

ipsec_vpn.notes: Enumerate IKE transforms and check for aggressive mode pre-shared key cracking.

openvpn.notes: Identify OpenVPN version for known CVEs.

irc.notes: Check for Unreal IRCd backdoor (CVE-2010-2075). May be C2 channel indicator.

squid.notes: Open proxy can be used to pivot to internal network or bypass firewall controls.

socks.notes: Open SOCKS proxy enables pivoting into internal networks.

hadoop.notes: Unauthenticated HDFS can expose sensitive data. YARN RCE via job submission on port 8088.

spark.notes: Unauthenticated Spark master allows job submission = RCE.

jupyter.notes: Unauthenticated Jupyter = arbitrary code execution in notebook environment.

airflow.notes: Default creds: admin/admin. Connections and variables store plaintext credentials.

mqtt.notes: Subscribe to '#' wildcard to capture all topics. Port 8883 is MQTTS. Often no auth.

modbus.notes: ICS/SCADA protocol. No authentication. Read/write coils and registers with caution.

dnp3.notes: Industrial control system protocol. Treat with extreme care in production environments.

bacnet.notes: Building automation systems (HVAC, access control). Often no authentication.

wsdiscovery.notes: Discovers WS-Discovery enabled devices (printers, cameras, Windows machines).

upnp.notes: UPnP can expose internal device info and allow port forwarding manipulation.

nping_msexchange.notes: Check for ProxyLogon/ProxyShell (CVE-2021-26855, CVE-2021-34473). Enumerate OWA/ECP/EWS.

sharepoint.notes: Check for CVE-2022-29108, CVE-2023-29357. Enumerate lists and documents via API.

citrix.notes: Check for Citrix Bleed (CVE-2023-4966), CVE-2019-19781 (Shitrix path traversal).

fortigate.notes: Check for CVE-2022-40684, CVE-2023-27997. Enumerate VPN portals and management interfaces.

paloalto.notes: Check for CVE-2024-3400 (OS command injection). Enumerate GP portal.

aria2.notes: Unauthenticated RPC allows arbitrary file download and SSRF.

webmin.notes: CVE-2019-15107 (backdoor), CVE-2019-12840 (RCE). Check for default creds.

printer.notes: Printers can store print jobs with sensitive data. PRET enables advanced printer exploitation.

http_apache.notes: Check for mod_status, mod_info exposure. Test for path traversal on 2.4.49-2.4.50.

http_nginx.notes: Check for stub_status exposure, alias traversal, off-by-slash misconfig.

http_iis.notes: Check for short name brute force (8.3 filenames), WebDAV, and .config disclosure.

http_tomcat.notes: Default creds on /manager are very common. Check for WAR file upload via manager.

http_wordpress.notes: WPScan is the go-to. Check xmlrpc.php for brute-force amplification. Enumerate plugins.

http_joomla.notes: Check for exposed admin panel, config backup, and API info disclosure.

http_drupal.notes: CHANGELOG.txt reveals exact version. Drupalgeddon (SA-CORE-2018-002) is critical.

http_weblogic.notes: T3 protocol deserialization and console exposure are common attack vectors.

http_jboss.notes: JMX console with default creds is a classic finding. Check for deserialization via invoker.

ssh_openssh.notes: ssh-audit gives detailed algorithm/vulnerability analysis. Check for user enumeration on older versions.

smb_windows.notes: Always check for EternalBlue (MS17-010). Null session enumeration is high value.

smb_samba.notes: Check for SambaCry (CVE-2017-7494) on versions 3.5.0-4.6.4. Null sessions common on misconfigured Samba.

mssql_specific.notes: sa with blank password is classic. xp_cmdshell gives RCE if enabled.

mysql_specific.notes: Root with no password is common on dev instances. Check for UDF exploitation.

postgres_specific.notes: Default user is postgres. Check pg_hba.conf for trust auth. COPY TO/FROM gives file read/write.

ftp_vsftpd.notes: vsftpd 2.3.4 has a famous backdoor (CVE-2011-2523). Always test with nmap script.

ftp_proftpd.notes: ProFTPD 1.3.3c has a backdoor. mod_copy allows unauthenticated file copy on 1.3.5.

rdp_windows.notes: Check for BlueKeep (CVE-2019-0708) on older Windows. NLA bypass is valuable.

ldap_ad.notes: AS-REP roasting and Kerberoasting are high-value AD attacks. Enumerate naming contexts first.

http_exchange.notes: ProxyShell and ProxyLogon are critical Exchange vulns. OWA often leaks domain info via NTLM.

http_phpmyadmin.notes: Setup page without auth is a common misconfiguration. Check for default root/empty password.

http_webmin_product.notes: CVE-2019-15107 allows unauthenticated RCE via password_change.cgi on Webmin 1.890-1.920.
